title: Lebanon Emerges as Weak Link in U.S.-Iran Deal to End War
summary: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, once seen as a secondary front to the American-Israeli war on Iran, has become one of the main obstacles to ending it.

title: Mines, Logistics and Deep Uncertainty Threaten a Middle East Oil Rebound
summary: More oil is getting out of the Persian Gulf, but the region’s producers are looking for signs that it is safe as they ramp up plans for alternative routes.

title: Trump’s Iran Deal Is Off to a Chaotic Start
summary: The preliminary agreement, not yet a week old, has sown confusion: nixed Swiss signing ceremonies, rifts with Israel and Congress, and uncertainty over new talks.

title: Disability Groups Fear RFK Jr.’s New Special Education Role
summary: Alienated by Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s claims about autism, advocates for disabled students are sounding the alarm about the Trump administration's shifting special education programs to his department.

title: The Reflecting Pool Appears to Be Rejecting Its Makeover
summary: As National Park Service crews try to clean the algae that turned the water bright green, another problem has developed: The “American flag blue” coating is coming off.

title: ‘A Literal Coverup’: What Is the Kennedy Center Hiding Behind Those Tarps?
summary: The arts center says it pulled President Trump’s name from the building front. But the result remains hidden by tarps, prompting questions and speculation.

title: Democratic Socialists Took City Hall. Now They’re Aiming at Congress.
summary: After propelling Zohran Mamdani to become mayor of New York City, the Democratic Socialists of America are trying to take down incumbents in the House and the State Legislature.

title: Arizona Democrat’s Tough Past Fuels Pitch for a Key House Seat
summary: JoAnna Mendoza, a Democrat challenging Representative Juan Ciscomani in a tossup district, is leaning into her painful and unique life story to appeal to voters.

title: Trump Shifts Stance in South Carolina After Losses in Governor’s Races
summary: On Friday the president made the unusual move of endorsing both candidates in the Republican runoff for South Carolina governor, after initially supporting one of them.

title: Trump Called Iván Cepeda a ‘Radical Left Marxist.’ Can He Be Colombia’s President?
summary: Iván Cepeda was caught off guard by a right-wing outsider and anti-leftist anger. He tells The Times he is not giving up.

title: Abelardo De La Espriella, Right-Wing Outsider, Could Be Colombia’s Next President
summary: President Trump endorsed Abelardo De La Espriella, the conservative presidential candidate in Sunday’s election who has strong U.S. ties.

title: Memo by Rubio Approved Detention of Immigrant Who Criticized Trump Ally
summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Beto Coral’s criticism of a presidential candidate in his home country of Colombia undermined U.S. foreign policy.

title: Danny McBride Thinks Men Learned All the Wrong Lessons From Movies
summary: The writer and actor, known for his profane comedic antiheroes, likes to find universal truths in human flaws.

title: Creature From the Green Lagoon
summary: Trump’s slimy, stinky swamp within the swamp.

title: Will the Real JD Vance Please Stand Up?
summary: Three Opinion writers share their views on “Communion,” the vice president’s new book.

title: Game Change
summary: New York City’s temporary transformation by the Knicks’ resurgence offers a glimpse of a kinder, more connected world.

title: After Andy Burnham’s Win, the UK Is Set to Test How Far Charisma Can Shift Electoral Dynamics
summary: The governing Labour Party may oust Prime Minister Keir Starmer in favor of the more popular Andy Burnham — who will still face all the same challenges.
